title: First Transcontinental Railroad opens
subtitle: Rail line in the United States
fact: America's first transcontinental railroad was a 1,911-mile (3,075 km) continuous railroad line built between 1863 and 1869 that connected the existing eastern U.S. rail network at Council Bluffs, Iowa, with the Pacific coast at the Oakland Long Wharf on San Francisco Bay.

title: Trans-Siberian railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Russia
fact: The Trans-Siberian Railway, historically known as the Great Siberian Route and often shortened to Transsib, is a large railway system that connects European Russia to the Russian Far East.

title: Yamanote Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Tokyo, Japan
fact: The Yamanote Line is a railway loop service in Tokyo, Japan, operated by the East Japan Railway Company.

title: Docklands Light Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Greater London, United Kingdom
fact: The Docklands Light Railway (DLR) is an automated light metro system primarily serving the redeveloped Docklands area of London and providing a direct connection between London's two major financial districts, Canary Wharf and the City of London.

title: High Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Manhattan, the United States
fact: The High Line is a 1.45-mile-long (2.33 km) elevated linear park, greenway, and rail trail created on a former New York Central Railroad spur on the West Side of Manhattan in New York City.

title: Burma Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Ban Pong, Thailand
fact: The Burma Railway, also known as the Siam–Burma Railway, Thai–Burma Railway and similar names, or as the Death Railway, is a 415 km (258 mi) railway between Ban Pong, Thailand, and Thanbyuzayat, Burma.

title: Shanghai maglev train opens
subtitle: Rail line in Pudong, China
fact: The Shanghai maglev train (SMT) or Shanghai Transrapid is a magnetic levitation train (maglev) line that operates in Shanghai, China.

title: High Speed 1 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Essex, United Kingdom
fact: High Speed 1 (HS1), officially the Channel Tunnel Rail Link (CTRL), is a 109.9-kilometre (68.3-mile) high-speed railway linking London with the Channel Tunnel.

title: Northern line opens
subtitle: Rail line in London Borough of Wandsworth, United Kingdom
fact: The Northern line is a London Underground line that runs between North London and South London.

title: Tōkaidō Shinkansen opens
subtitle: Rail line in Tokyo, Japan
fact: The Tōkaidō Shinkansen is a Japanese high-speed rail line and part of the nationwide Shinkansen network.

title: Piccadilly line opens
subtitle: Rail line in London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham, United Kingdom
fact: The Piccadilly line is a deep-level London Underground line which runs between the west and the north of London with 53 stations on the line.

title: Jubilee line opens
subtitle: Rail line in London, United Kingdom
fact: The Jubilee line is a London Underground line that runs between Stanmore in suburban north-west London and Stratford in east London, via the West End, South Bank and Docklands.

title: Taiwan High Speed Rail opens
subtitle: Rail line in Taipei, Taiwan
fact: Taiwan High Speed Rail (THSR) is a high-speed railway network in Taiwan, which consists of a single line that runs approximately 350 km (217 mi) along the western coast of the island, from Taipei in the north to the southern city of Kaohsiung.

title: Bakerloo line opens
subtitle: Rail line in London, United Kingdom
fact: The Bakerloo line is a London Underground line that runs between Harrow & Wealdstone in suburban north-west London and Elephant & Castle in south London, via the West End.

title: Central line opens
subtitle: Rail line in London, United Kingdom
fact: The Central line is a London Underground line that runs between West Ruislip or Ealing Broadway in the west, and Epping or Woodford via Hainault in the north-east, via the West End, the City, and the East End.

title: Metropolitan line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Hertfordshire, United Kingdom
fact: The Metropolitan line, colloquially known as the Met, is a London Underground line between Aldgate in the City of London and Amersham and Chesham in Buckinghamshire, with branches to Watford in Hertfordshire and Uxbridge in Hillingdon.

title: District line opens
subtitle: Rail line in London Borough of Richmond upon Thames, United Kingdom
fact: The District line is a London Underground line running from Upminster in the east and Edgware Road in northwestern Central London to Earl's Court in west London, where it splits into multiple branches.

title: Hejaz Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Saudi Arabia
fact: The Hejaz railway was a narrow-gauge railway that ran from Damascus to Medina, through the Hejaz region of modern-day Saudi Arabia, with a branch line to Haifa on the Mediterranean Sea.

title: BTS Skytrain opens
subtitle: Rail line in Bangkok, Konbaung dynasty
fact: The Bangkok Mass Transit System, commonly known as the BTS Skytrain, is an elevated rapid transit system in Bangkok, Thailand.

title: Circle line opens
subtitle: Rail line in London, United Kingdom
fact: The Circle line is a spiral-shaped London Underground line, running from Hammersmith in the west to Edgware Road and then looping around central London back to Edgware Road.

title: Victoria line opens
subtitle: Rail line in London, United Kingdom
fact: The Victoria line is a London Underground line that runs between Brixton in South London, and Walthamstow Central in the east, via the West End.

title: Staten Island Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Staten Island, the United States
fact: The Staten Island Railway (SIR) is a rapid transit line in the New York City borough of Staten Island.

title: Tōhoku Shinkansen opens
subtitle: Rail line in Tokyo, Japan
fact: The Tōhoku Shinkansen is a Japanese high-speed rail line and part of the nationwide Shinkansen network.

title: Benguela railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Angola
fact: The Benguela Railway is a Cape gauge railway line that runs through Angola from west to east, being the largest and most important railway line in the country.

title: Tōkaidō Main Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Tokyo, Japan
fact: The Tōkaidō Main Line is one of the most important railway corridors in Japan, connecting the major cities of Tokyo and Kobe via Shizuoka, Nagoya, Kyoto and Osaka.

title: Hokkaido Shinkansen opens
subtitle: Rail line in Aomori Prefecture, Japan
fact: The Hokkaido Shinkansen is a Japanese high-speed Shinkansen rail line operated by the Hokkaido Railway Company that connects with the Tōhoku Shinkansen in northern Aomori Prefecture on Honshu and extends into Hokkaido through the undersea Seikan Tunnel.

title: Haramain high-speed railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Saudi Arabia
fact: The Haramain High Speed Railway, is a passenger railway serving the Mecca and Medina provinces in western Saudi Arabia.

title: Darjeeling Himalayan Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Jalpaiguri district, India
fact: The Darjeeling Himalayan Railway, also known as the DHR or the Toy Train, is a 610 mm gauge railway that runs between New Jalpaiguri and Darjeeling in the Indian state of West Bengal.

title: Waterloo & City line opens
subtitle: Rail line in London, United Kingdom
fact: The Waterloo & City line, colloquially known as The Drain, is a shuttle line of the London Underground that runs between Waterloo and Bank stations with no intermediate stops.

title: Mauritania Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Mauritania
fact: The Mauritania Railway is the national railway of Mauritania.

title: Hokuriku Shinkansen opens
subtitle: Rail line in Nagano Prefecture, Japan
fact: The Hokuriku Shinkansen is a high-speed Shinkansen railway line connecting Tokyo with Tsuruga in the Hokuriku region of Japan.

title: Baghdad Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Syria
fact: The Baghdad railway, also known as the Berlin–Baghdad railway, was started in 1903 to connect Berlin with the then Ottoman city of Baghdad, from where the Germans wanted to establish a port on the Persian Gulf, with a 1,600-kilometre (1,000 mi) line through modern-day Turkey, Syria, and Iraq.

title: Talyllyn Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Tywyn, United Kingdom
fact: The Talyllyn Railway is a narrow-gauge railway in Wales running for 7+1⁄4 miles (12 km) from Tywyn on the Mid-Wales coast to Nant Gwernol near the village of Abergynolwyn.

title: Beijing–Shanghai high-speed railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Beijing, China
fact: The Beijing–Shanghai high-speed railway is a high-speed railway that connects two major economic zones in the People's Republic of China: the Bohai Economic Rim and the Yangtze River Delta.

title: San'yō Shinkansen opens
subtitle: Rail line in Osaka Prefecture, Japan
fact: The San'yō Shinkansen is a line of the Japanese Shinkansen high-speed rail network, connecting Shin-Osaka in Osaka with Hakata Station in Fukuoka, the two largest cities in western Japan.

title: Stockton and Darlington Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in County Durham, United Kingdom
fact: The Stockton and Darlington Railway (S&DR) was a railway company that operated in north-east England from 1825 to 1863.

title: Asakusa Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Tokyo, Japan
fact: The Toei Asakusa Line is a subway line in Tokyo, Japan, operated by the municipal subway operator Toei Subway.

title: Hammersmith & City line opens
subtitle: Rail line in London Borough of Hammersmith and Fulham, United Kingdom
fact: The Hammersmith & City line is a London Underground line that runs between Hammersmith in west London and Barking in east London.

title: Ginza Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Tokyo, Japan
fact: The Ginza Line, officially designated as Line 3 Ginza Line, is a subway line in Tokyo, Japan, operated by Tokyo Metro.

title: East–West MRT line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Singapore
fact: The East–West Line (EWL) is a high-capacity Mass Rapid Transit (MRT) line operated by SMRT in Singapore, running from Pasir Ris station in the east to Tuas Link station in the west, with an additional branch between Changi Airport and Tanah Merah stations.

title: Seoul Metropolitan Subway Line 1 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Seoul, South Korea
fact: Seoul Subway Line 1 of the Seoul Metropolitan Subway is a rapid transit and commuter rail line which links central Seoul, South Korea to Yeoncheon in the northeast, Incheon in the southwest, and Sinchang via Suwon and Cheonan in the south.

title: Nilgiri Mountain Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Tamil Nadu, India
fact: The Nilgiri Mountain Railway (NMR), colloquially called the "Toy Train" by locals, is a 1,000 mm metre gauge railway in Nilgiris district, Tamil Nadu, India, built by the British in 1908.

title: Liverpool and Manchester Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Lancashire, United Kingdom
fact: The Liverpool and Manchester Railway (L&MR) was the first inter-city railway in the world.

title: Marunouchi Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Tokyo, Japan
fact: The Marunouchi Line is a subway line in Tokyo, Japan, operated by Tokyo Metro.

title: North–South MRT line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Singapore
fact: The North–South Line (NSL) is a high-capacity Mass Rapid Transit (MRT) line in Singapore.

title: Paris Métro Line 14 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: Paris Métro Line 14 is one of the sixteen lines currently open on the Paris Métro.

title: Qingzang railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in China
fact: The Qinghai–Tibet railway or Qingzang railway, is a high-elevation railway line in China between Xining, Qinghai Province, and Lhasa, Tibet.

title: East London Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in London Borough of Croydon, United Kingdom
fact: The East London line is a railway line running north to south through the East, Docklands and South areas of London.

title: Amsterdam Central station opens
subtitle: Rail line in Amsterdam, Netherlands
fact: Amsterdam Centraal station is the largest railway station in Amsterdam, North Holland, the Netherlands.

title: Trans-Iranian Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Golestan Province, Iran
fact: The Trans-Iranian Railway was a major railway building project started in Pahlavi Iran in 1927 and completed in 1938, under the direction of the then-Iranian monarch Reza Shah.

title: Chinese Eastern Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Heilongjiang, China
fact: The Chinese Eastern Railway or CER, is the historical name for a railway system in Northeast China.

title: TAZARA Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Zambia
fact: The Tazara Railway, also called the Uhuru Railway or the Tanzam Railway, is a railway in East Africa linking the port of Dar es Salaam in east Tanzania with the town of Kapiri Mposhi in Zambia's Central Province.

title: Marmaray opens
subtitle: Rail line in Kocaeli Province, Turkey
fact: Marmaray is a 76.6-kilometre-long (47.6 mi) commuter rail line located in Istanbul, Turkey.

title: Kyushu Shinkansen opens
subtitle: Rail line in Kagoshima Prefecture, Japan
fact: The Kyushu Shinkansen is a Japanese high-speed rail line and part of the nationwide Shinkansen network.

title: Seoul Subway Line 2 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Seoul, South Korea
fact: Seoul Subway Line 2, also known as the Circle Line, is a circular line of the Seoul Metropolitan Subway.

title: RER B opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: RER B is one of the five lines in the Réseau Express Régional, a hybrid commuter rail and rapid transit system serving Paris, France and its Île-de-France suburbs.

title: Hibiya Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Tokyo, Japan
fact: The Hibiya Line is a subway line in Tokyo, Japan, owned and operated by Tokyo Metro.

title: Panama Canal Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Panama
fact: The Panama Canal Railway is a railway line linking the Atlantic Ocean to the Pacific Ocean in Central America.

title: Kalka–Shimla Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Himachal Pradesh, India
fact: The Kalka–Shimla Railway is a 2 ft 6 in narrow-gauge railway in North India which traverses a mostly mountainous route from Kalka to Shimla.

title: Paris Métro Line 1 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: Paris Métro Line 1 is one of the sixteen lines of the Paris Métro.

title: Baikal-Amur Mainline opens
subtitle: Rail line in Irkutsk Oblast, Russia
fact: The Baikal–Amur Mainline is a 1,520 mm broad-gauge railway line in Russia.

title: RER A opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: RER A is one of the five lines in the Réseau Express Régional, a hybrid commuter rail and rapid transit system serving the city and suburbs of Paris, France.

title: Ascensor da Glória opens
subtitle: Rail line in Santa Maria Maior, Portugal
fact: The Glória Funicular is a hybrid funicular-tram line in the civil parish of Santo António, in Lisbon, Portugal, operated by Carris.

title: Jungfrau railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Grindelwald, Switzerland
fact: The Jungfrau Railway is a mountain rack railway in the Bernese Alps, Switzerland, connecting Kleine Scheidegg in the Bernese Oberland to the Jungfraujoch, across the Valais border.

title: Lima and Callao Subway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Lima, Peru
fact: The Lima and Callao Metro is a rapid transit system that serves the cities of Lima and Callao, which make up the Lima metropolitan area.

title: Semmering railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Lower Austria, First Republic of Austria
fact: The Semmering railway in Austria, which starts at Gloggnitz and leads over the Semmering to Mürzzuschlag, was the first mountain railway in Europe built with a standard gauge track.

title: Jōetsu Shinkansen opens
subtitle: Rail line in Saitama Prefecture, Japan
fact: The Jōetsu Shinkansen is a Japanese high-speed rail line and part of the nationwide Shinkansen network.

title: Bergen Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Norway
fact: The Bergen Line, or the Bergen Railway, is a 371-kilometre (231 mi) long scenic standard gauge railway line between Bergen and Hønefoss, Norway.

title: Budapest Metro Line 1 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Budapest, Hungary
fact: Line 1 is the oldest line of the Budapest Metro, built from 1894 to 1896.

title: Tōzai Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Tokyo, Japan
fact: The Tōzai Line is a rapid transit line in Tokyo and Chiba Prefecture, Japan, owned and operated by Tokyo Metro.

title: Paris Métro Line 4 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: Line 4 is one of the sixteen currently opened lines of the Paris Métro, as well as one of its three fully automated lines.

title: RER C opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: RER C is one of the five lines in the Réseau Express Régional, a hybrid commuter rail and rapid transit system serving Paris and its suburbs.

title: Fukutoshin Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Tokyo, Japan
fact: The Fukutoshin Line, formally the No.

title: Addis Ababa–Djibouti Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Addis Ababa, Djibouti
fact: The Addis Ababa–Djibouti Railway is a standard gauge international railway that serves as the backbone of the new Ethiopian National Railway Network.

title: Chiyoda Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Tokyo, Japan
fact: The Chiyoda Line is a subway line owned and operated by Tokyo Metro in Tokyo, Japan.

title: Tünel opens
subtitle: Rail line in Beyoğlu, Turkey
fact: The Tünel is a historic underground rubber-tyred funicular line in Istanbul, Turkey.

title: Trans-Mongolian Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Mongolia
fact: The Trans-Mongolian Railway connects Ulan-Ude on the Trans-Siberian Railway in Buryatia, Russia, with Ulanchab in Inner Mongolia, China, via Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia.

title: Vatican Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Vatican City
fact: The Vatican Railway was opened in 1934 to serve Vatican City and its only station, Vatican City.

title: Salekhard–Igarka Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Soviet Union
fact: The 1,524 mm broad gauge Salekhard–Igarka Railway is an incomplete railway in northern Siberia.

title: U5 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Berlin, Germany
fact: U5 is a line on the Berlin U-Bahn.

title: Carmelit opens
subtitle: Rail line in Haifa, Israel
fact: The Carmelit is an underground funicular railway in Haifa, Israel.

title: Yūrakuchō Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Tokyo, Japan
fact: The Yūrakuchō Line is a subway line in Japan owned and operated by Tokyo Metro.

title: Paris Métro Line 6 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: Line 6 is one of the sixteen currently open lines of the Paris Métro rapid transit system.

title: RER E opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: RER E is one of the five lines in the Réseau Express Régional, a hybrid commuter rail and rapid transit system serving Paris and its suburbs.

title: Baku–Tbilisi–Kars railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Azerbaijan, Georgia
fact: The Baku–Tbilisi–Kars (BTK), or Baku–Tbilisi–Akhalkalaki–Kars railway (BTAK), is a railway connecting Azerbaijan, Georgia and Turkey, which became operational on 30 October 2017 following several years of delays.

title: Pilatus railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Alpnach, Switzerland
fact: The Pilatus Railway is a mountain railway in Switzerland and the steepest rack railway in the world, with a maximum gradient of 48% and an average gradient of 35%.

title: Coast Tram opens
subtitle: Rail line in West Flanders, Belgium
fact: The Coast Tram is a light rail service connecting the cities and towns along the Belgian coast between De Panne, near the French border, and Knokke-Heist, near the Dutch border.

title: Belgrade–Bar railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Serbia
fact: The Belgrade–Bar railway is a 476.59 km (296.14 mi) long electrified main line connecting the Serbian capital of Belgrade with the town of Bar, a major seaport in Montenegro.

title: Paris Métro Line 11 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: Paris Métro Line 11 is one of the sixteen currently open lines of the Paris Métro.

title: Paris Métro Line 10 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: Paris Métro Line 10 is one of the sixteen currently open métro lines in Paris, France.

title: Paris Métro Line 8 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: Paris Métro Line 8 is one of the sixteen lines of the Paris Métro currently opened.

title: U2 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Berlin, Germany
fact: U2 is a line of the Berlin U-Bahn.

title: RER D opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: RER D is one of the five lines in the Réseau Express Régional, a hybrid commuter rail and rapid transit system serving Paris and its suburbs.

title: Paris Métro Line 13 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: Paris Métro Line 13 is one of the sixteen lines of the Paris Métro that are currently open.

title: Paris Métro Line 12 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: Paris Métro Line 12 is one of the sixteen lines currently open on the Paris Métro.

title: Gotthard railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Canton of Lucerne, Switzerland
fact: The Gotthard railway is the Swiss trans-alpine railway line from northern Switzerland to the canton of Ticino.

title: Paris Métro Line 2 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: Paris Métro Line 2 is one of the sixteen currently open lines of the Paris Métro.

title: Paris Métro Line 7 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: Paris Métro Line 7 is one of sixteen currently open lines of the Paris Métro system, which links La Courneuve–8 mai 1945 in Seine-Saint-Denis northeast of Paris, with Mairie d'Ivry southeast and Villejuif–Louis Aragon in the south, while passing through important parts of central Paris on a northeast to diagonal to south route.

title: Berlin Ringbahn opens
subtitle: Rail line in Berlin, Germany
fact: The Ringbahn is a 37.5 km (23.3 mi) long circle route around Berlin's inner city area, on the Berlin S-Bahn network.

title: Paris Métro Line 9 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: Paris Métro Line 9 is one of the sixteen lines of the Paris Métro network currently open.

title: Bernina railway line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Canton of the Grisons, Switzerland
fact: The Bernina railway line is a single-track 1,000 mm metre gauge railway line forming part of the Rhaetian Railway (RhB).

title: Bolshaya Koltsevaya Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Moscow, Russia
fact: The Bolshaya Koltsevaya line is a rapid transit line of the Moscow Metro system in Moscow, Russia.

title: Gornergrat railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Zermatt, Switzerland
fact: The Gornergrat Railway is a mountain rack railway, located in the Swiss canton of Valais.

title: Yokohama Municipal Subway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Yokohama, Japan
fact: Yokohama Municipal Subway is a rapid transit system that serves Yokohama in Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan, and is operated by the Yokohama City Transportation Bureau.

title: Köln–Frankfurt high-speed rail line opens
subtitle: Rail line in North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany
fact: The Cologne–Frankfurt high-speed rail line is a 180-kilometre-long (110 mi) high-speed line in Germany, connecting the cities of Cologne and Frankfurt.

title: U7 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Berlin, Germany
fact: The U7 is a rail line on the Berlin U-Bahn.

title: U6 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Berlin, Germany
fact: U6 is a 19.9 km (12.4 mi) long rapid transit line on the Berlin U-Bahn with 29 stations.

title: Karasuma Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Kyoto, Japan
fact: The Karasuma Line is one of the two lines of the Kyoto Municipal Subway operated by Kyoto Municipal Transportation Bureau in Kyoto, Japan.

title: Moscow Monorail opens
subtitle: Rail line in Moscow, Russia
fact: The Moscow Monorail was a 4.7-kilometre-long (2.9 mi) monorail line located in the North-Eastern Administrative Okrug of Moscow, Russia.

title: Paris Métro Line 5 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: Paris Métro Line 5 is one of the sixteen currently opened lines of the Paris Métro in Paris, France.

title: Paris Métro Line 3 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: Paris Métro Line 3 is one of the sixteen currently open lines of the Paris Métro.

title: Beijing–Tianjin intercity railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Beijing, China
fact: The Beijing–Tianjin intercity railway is a Chinese high-speed railway that runs 117 kilometres (72.7 mi) line between Beijing and Tianjin.

title: Kaluzhsko-Rizhskaya Line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Moscow, Russia
fact: The Kaluzhsko-Rizhskaya line is a line of the Moscow Metro, that originally existed as two separate radial lines, Rizhskaya and Kaluzhskaya opened in 1958 and 1962, respectively.

title: Paris Métro Line 3bis opens
subtitle: Rail line in Paris, France
fact: Paris Métro Line 3bis is one of the sixteen currently open lines of the Paris Métro.

title: Moscow-Saint Petersburg Railway opens
subtitle: Rail line in Moscow, Russia
fact: The Saint Petersburg to Moscow railway runs for 649.7 kilometers (403.7 mi) through four oblasts: Leningrad, Novgorod, Tver and Moscow.

title: Sokolnicheskaya line opens
subtitle: Rail line in Moscow, Russia
fact: The Sokolnicheskaya line (Russian: Соко́льническая ли́ния, IPA: [sɐˈkolʲnʲitɕɪskəjə ˈlʲinʲɪjə], formerly Kirovsko-Frunzenskaya is a line of the Moscow Metro in Moscow, Russia.

title: Barcelona Metro line 5 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Barcelona, Spain
fact: Line 5, often known as "Línia Blava", is a rapid transit line of the Barcelona Metro network, operated by TMB and part of the ATM fare-integrated transport network.

title: U8 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Berlin, Germany
fact: U8 is a line on the Berlin U-Bahn.

title: U1 opens
subtitle: Rail line in Berlin, Germany
fact: U1 is a line on the Berlin U-Bahn, which is 8.8 kilometres (5.5 mi) long and has 13 stations.
